assessmentCatalog.title: Assessments

settings.pageDesc: Manage your data, export your results, and control your anonymity.

accountIdentity.title: Account Identity
accountIdentity.desc: MindPolis is designed to be fully functional even when anonymous.
accountIdentity.emailNote: Your email is only used for account recovery and important notifications. We do not sell or share this data.

dataPortability.title: Data Portability
dataPortability.desc: Export all your historical assessment data and results in JSON format.

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.desc: Permanently delete your account and all associated assessment data.
dangerZone.warning: This action cannot be undone. All data will be immediately and permanently erased from our servers in accordance with strict privacy standards.

hero.subtitle: MindPolis measures where you stand across 8 dimensions — the real axes of modern political disagreement. Built on political psychology research, not internet quizzes.

problem.label: The problem
problem.title1: The Political Compass reduces politics to 2 axes.
problem.title2: Real political cognition has at least 8.
problem.body: Most political tests ask whether you agree with statements designed to confirm your existing label. MindPolis presents genuine trade-off scenarios — situations where no option is purely correct — to reveal the underlying value structures that actually drive political orientation. The result is a profile, not a position.

list.label: Economic Organization
list.description: Who should control the allocation of goods and capital? How should production, distribution, and ownership be structured in a society?

list.label: Authority & Liberty
list.description: How should society balance individual freedoms against collective order? Where does legitimate authority end and personal autonomy begin?

list.label: Tradition & Change
list.description: How should societies relate to inherited institutions, norms, and practices? Is continuity a virtue or an obstacle to justice?

list.label: Nationalism & Globalism
list.description: Where does legitimate political authority end — at the national border or beyond it? How should sovereignty relate to international integration?

list.label: Justice Model
list.description: What is the purpose of punishment and how should wrongs be addressed? Does justice require retribution, restoration, or prevention?

list.label: Ecology & Growth
list.description: How should societies balance environmental limits with economic development? Is growth compatible with sustainability, or fundamentally in tension with it?

list.label: Institutional Trust
list.description: Which systems of governance deserve our confidence and deference? How much authority should be delegated to formal institutions?

list.label: Diversity & Cohesion
list.description: How should societies manage cultural difference and social unity? Is pluralism a source of strength or of fragmentation?

steps.title: Scenario-based
steps.body: You're presented with realistic policy trade-offs and dilemmas — not loaded statements. No purely correct answer, only value-revealing choices.

steps.title: Cross-axis scoring
steps.body: Multiple dimensions are measured simultaneously. This detects inconsistency patterns and provides a highly nuanced profile.

steps.title: Instant profile
steps.body: Your results are scored immediately in the browser. Create an optional free account afterward to save and track your results over time.

whatYouGet.label: What you get
whatYouGet.title: Beyond labels and tribal lines.
whatYouGet.body: Instead of reducing your worldview to a single dot on a 2D compass, you receive a rigorous breakdown of the structural morals and socio-economic frameworks that inform your choices.

items.title: Dimensional Breakdown
items.body: See precisely where you land across all 8 major axes, from economic organization to institutional trust.

items.title: Nuance & Contradictions
items.body: Discover where your values challenge standard partisan dogmas and where you hold conflicting moral priorities.

items.title: Actionable Summaries
items.body: Read generated executive summaries that capture the essence of your political cognition in a few paragraphs.

items.title: Historical Tracking
items.body: View your past assessment data chronologically to analyze how your fundamental values evolve over time.

methodology.label: Methodology
methodology.cta: Read more about our scientific framework →

items.title: Academic grounding
items.body: The framework draws from Haidt's Moral Foundations Theory, Inglehart's World Values Survey dimensions, Jost's political cognition research, and Stenner's authoritarianism studies.

items.title: Current limitations
items.body: MindPolis v0.1 is a theory-informed instrument, not yet a formally validated psychometric tool. It lacks factor analysis or test-retest reliability measurement.

items.title: Scoring design
items.body: Each question option carries weighted scores for its primary axis (±2) and secondary axes (±1). Final axis scores are normalized to [−1, +1].

items.title: What it is not
items.body: MindPolis does not tell you your "correct" political position, predict voting behavior, or classify you into a party affiliation. It maps the underlying value structures.

bottomCta.body: No sign-up required. Takes under 10 minutes. Create a free account afterward to save your results.

assessmentIntro.instructionsBody: This assessment presents real-world policy dilemmas. There are no correct answers. Respond according to your genuine judgment, not what seems expected.

take.howConfident: HOW CONFIDENT ARE YOU IN THIS JUDGMENT?
